data.title: Sectra AB (publ) reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Sectra AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:STKTF): Q2 profit of SEK 87.8 million.
  • Net sales of SEK 753.5 million.
  • The company's order bookings for Q2 surpassed SEK 4 billion.

data.title: Trevi stock rockets on Haduvio study update
data.description:
Trevi Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TRVI) shares shot up as much as 81% Thursday after the company said it won’t need to change the sample size for an ongoing Phase 2b study of its drug Haduvio for chronic cough.

The company said the independent data statistician for the clinical trial has reaffirmed the study’s current sample size of N=160. The analysis was conducted on the highest dose in the study, 108mg twice daily.

The study, called CORAL, is evaluating three dose levels of Haduvio, also known as oral nalbuphine ER, for the treatment of chronic cough in patients with idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. Trevi (NASDAQ:TRVI) said the study has reached 75% enrollment, with topline results expected in the first half of 2025, according to a statement.

Trevi (TRVI) added that it was pleased by the outcome of the sample size re-estimation analysis as it supports continued execution of the trial as originally planned and confirms key powering assumptions in the trial’s design.

Earlier this month, Trevi (TRVI) shares tumbled post-market after the company reported topline results from a human abuse potential study of Haduvio.

data.title: WFE 2025 outlook cut lower due to China, lower DRAM demand: Jefferies
data.description:
Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) and KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) price targets were reduced by Jefferies due to a combination of weaker wafer fab equipment from China and a slowdown in memory demand.

"We are cutting our CY25 WFE outlook further to reflect a lower estimate for China (now 20% of WFE from 30%+) and less strength in DRAM," said Jefferies analysts, led by Blayne Curtis, in a note. "From our perspective, the only real strength is from TSMC (NYSE:TSM) at the leading edge, but there are questions around Foundry strategy at both Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC)."

Jefferies reduced its price targets on Lam Research to $90 from $95 and KLA to $770 from $875, while retaining a Buy rating on both stocks. However, the investment firm slightly increased its price target on Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) to $185 from $180 while maintaining a Hold rating.

"While we do still see some longer-term upside in the group and recognize this is a group that investors will want to revisit, we struggle with the setup into January earnings, as estimates still need to come down," Curtis added.

Lam Research looks to have the most near-term upside of this group due to stronger NAND demand.

Jefferies now expects 5% WFE growth in calendar year 2024, zero growth in 2025 and 6% growth in 2026.

data.title: Synchrony credit card deliquency rate holds, charge-offs dip in November
data.description:
Synchrony Financial's (NYSE:SYF) credit metrics for its card business held steady in November, with its delinquency rate unchanged from October and its net charge-off rate improving.

The November delinquency rate was 4.9%, the same as in the previous month, but up from 4.7% a year ago. The rate was 30 basis points higher than 4.6% in November 2019, before the pandemic hit.

The adjusted net charge-off rate of 6.2% declined from 6.4% in October and increased from 5.6% a year ago. The rate still remains elevated from its prepandemic level of 4.9%.

Loan receivables of $102.9B at Nov. 30, 2024, rose 0.6% M/M and 1.7% Y/Y.

data.title: The cruise industry expected to ride a wave of growth for investors - analyst
data.description:
Consumer interest in cruises remained consistently strong in 2023 and 2024 and is expected to continue in 2025 and investors seeking strong growth characteristics, affordability, attractive valuation, and the tariff-free status of the cruise industry will continue to push multiples modestly higher.

According to industry data, the cruise industry is expected to see a 20% increase in the number of passengers to 30 million, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.

With that, Citigroup has maintained its Buy rating for the major players in the sector and raised its price target for Carnival Corp (NYSE:CCL) by 7% to $30, and by 10% for Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL) to $283 in respect of the increased appetite for cruise stocks.

For Carnival Corp (CCL), the largest of the three with 37% of the worldwide fleet, Citigroup’s James Hardiman believes that if the current levels of demand can persist going forward, the Carnival (CCL) model gets all the more powerful “given a minimalistic order book and the potential for outsized deleverage and return on invested capital.”

The second largest, Royal Caribbean (RCL), commands 22% of the worldwide fleet and 25% of worldwide capacity, and is the best performing of the three with a 104% gain in its stock price vs 32% for the S&P 500. While significant value was destroyed during the pandemic as the company struggled to keep its fleet afloat, RCL weathered the storm better than most, with the best momentum of the cruise industry headed into COVID. “We see no reason that this will not continue to be the case going forward given arguably the best assets in the space and a strong operational history of keeping costs in check,” Hardiman says, adding that RCL’s industry-leading yield/cost opportunity represents the best blend of risk and reward in the cruise industry.

“There is considerable upside to earnings” for Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NYSE:NCLH) as the company’s shift in strategy from “quality at all costs” to a more balanced yield/cost relationship gives Hardiman confidence that the considerable pricing opportunity ahead will not be offset by runaway costs. In Q3, NCLH earned a profit of $0.99 per share, an increase of 30% year-over-year, and 147% sequentially.

data.title: QT Imaging announces new commercial shipment in partnership with Couri Center
data.description:
• QT Imaging Holdings (NASDAQ:QTI) has announced expansion of locations offering the Breast Acoustic CTTM Scanner to Couri Center for Gynecology and Integrative Women's Health, in Peoria, IL.
  • The shipment was made together with its strategic business and distribution partner, NXC Imaging.
  • With the QTI Breast Acoustic CTTM System, patients can experience an enhanced approach to breast imaging that prioritizes comfort and safety without compromising image quality.
  • “Our commitment to bringing our safe and accurate breast imaging technology to more women continues to be realized. We could not be prouder of our newest partnership with Couri Center,” said Dr. Raluca Dinu, CEO at QTI.

data.title: Innoviz Technologies soars in delayed reaction to Mobileye development
data.description:
Innoviz Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:INVZ) jumped 27.0% in morning trading on Thursday, a day after Mobileye Global (MBLY) announced that it would integrate Innoviz LiDARs into its autonomous vehicle platform for testing and deployment with key customers.

The Mobileye Drive Platform is expected to empower a full suite of solutions, from Robotaxi, shuttles, public transport, ride pooling and autonomosu guided vehicles.

CEO Omer Keilaf said the collaboration with Mobileye (MBLY) on next-generation autonomous vehicle solutions that are both safer and more affordable signifies the shared commitment to accelerate AV adoption. "The agreement further attests to Innoviz's capability to meet the stringent requirements of OEMs worldwide and support L4 driving solutions that will help drive a new era of mobility," added Keilaf.

With the delayed rally, the LiDAR stock came within a penny of trading at $1.00 or higher for the first time since June.

data.title: Kenvue gains on report activist Starboard nominates five for board
data.description:
Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE) rose 1.3% after a report that activist Starboard Value has privately nominated five candidates to the Tylenol maker's board.

Starboard and Kenvue (KVUE) have been in discussions about the company's issues, according to a Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Starboard disclosed in October that the fund accumulated a stake and was pushing for changes to help the company's stock price. Kenvue needs to focus on fixing its skin, health and beauty segments, Jeff Smith, founder and CEO of Starboard Value said at the time after he presented at an activist conference.

Starboard highlighted in a presentation in October that it believes the skin, health and beauty segment, mainly known for its Neutrogena and Aveeno brands, can return its margins to FY19 levels, which would be in line with peers. Kenvue (KVUE) was spun off from Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) last year.

“The Kenvue board and management team are committed to acting in the best interests of the company and our shareholders, and in pursuit of this shared objective, we maintain ongoing dialogue with our shareholders,” a company spokesperson said in a statement to Bloomberg.

data.title: JPMorgan plans to hire 1,500 for hub in Argentina
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) plans to expand its Buenos Aires corporate center as one of its global hubs for technology, finance, operations, and legal services, hiring 1,500 workers over the next five years, according to a media report.

The global bank is "working on a longer-term real estate plan to accommodate" the new hires in addition to the 3,500 employees who already work for JPM in the Argentine capital, Alfonso Eyzaguirre, CEO of J.P. Morgan Latin America and Canada and head of Latin America investment banking, told Bloomberg News in an interview.

JPMorgan Chase's (NYSE:JPM) move to combine its commercial bank with its corporate and investment bank globally, will help the company grow in Latin America.

"Now that we’re totally integrated, we will be able to serve clients of the legacy commercial bank in countries in Latin America where we were not covering them,” Eyzaguirre said.

Before the integration, JPMorgan's (JPM) Latin American commercial bank business only operated in Brazil and Mexico. Its business, which serves businesses that range from startups to firms with more than $2B in annual revenue, can now deepen coverage in the region, he said.

J.P. Morgan has operated in Argentina for more than 125 years and has participated in most of the country's landmark transactions through advising local and international corporate clients and providing financing for the Argentine government, the company said on its website.

data.title: Wall Street slips after producer inflation data comes in hot
data.description:
U.S. stocks were largely lower on Thursday, following some key producer inflation data that came in hotter than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.14% in morning trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.27%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.30%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up nearly 2 basis points to 4.17%.

Before the start of regular trading, the November Producer Price Index (PPI) came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Separately, initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.2% after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.
